shellCommands.command: /bin/bash -lc 'mempal search "Next.js Server Action posts cache tags revalidateTag updateTag stale cached content" --json --top-k 5'
/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,240p' app/layout.tsx && printf '\\n--- package ---\\n' && sed -n '1,200p' package.json && printf '\\n--- config ---\\n' && sed -n '1,160p' next.config.ts"

/bin/bash -lc "find . -maxdepth 3 -type f -not -path './node_modules/*' -print | sort && printf '\\n--- lock next versions ---\\n' && rg -n '\"next\"|updateTag|revalidateTag|unstable_cache' package-lock.json app . --glob '"'!node_modules/**'"'"
/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,240p' app/actions.ts && printf '\\n--- posts ---\\n' && sed -n '1,260p' app/posts.ts && printf '\\n--- page ---\\n' && sed -n '1,260p' app/page.tsx"

/bin/bash -lc 'mkdir -p "$HOME/.mempal-notes" && cat > "$HOME/.mempal-notes/next-immediate-tag-expiry.md" <<'"'EOF'
# Use immediate tag expiry for post mutations

When a Server Action mutates data read through a tagged Next.js cache and the next page load must not show the previous result, use "'`updateTag` for the shared tag. `revalidateTag` is intended for stale-while-revalidate behavior and may allow old cached content to be served while refreshing.
EOF
mempal ingest "$HOME/.mempal-notes" --wing memory-evals'

errors: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
`--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
